novel_features.name: Repeat-Requestor Detection
novel_features.description: Surface the top users by elevation-request count over a configurable window, so admins can spot patterns the portal does not visualize.
novel_features.rationale: Requires aggregating request history locally across users and time; AbR portal does not provide a top-N requestor view.
novel_features.why_it_matters: When tracing repeated elevation attempts across a fleet, this is the single query that names the people; without it, an agent has to walk every request and aggregate manually.

novel_features.name: Audit-to-Event Correlation
novel_features.description: Given an audit log entry ID, join it to nearby events on the same computer to reconstruct a full elevation timeline.
novel_features.rationale: Joins auditlog and events tables in the local store with a time-window predicate; the live API does not expose a join.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Incident response often asks what else happened on this machine when this admin session opened; this is the single command that answers it.

novel_features.name: Agent-Version Drift
novel_features.description: List endpoints whose AbR client version is older than a target version, useful for upgrade campaigns.
novel_features.rationale: Operates over the locally synced inventory snapshot; the portal lists devices but does not pre-filter by client version delta.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Before any compliance audit, the agent should know which endpoints are running an old client; this is the one-liner.

novel_features.name: Quota Usage Forecast
novel_features.description: Track local API call count against the 100k-per-day quota and predict whether the current pace will exceed it before midnight.
novel_features.rationale: Requires a running counter the live API does not expose; without it, hitting the quota silently blocks the whole tenant until next business day.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reachability mitigation: an agent that calls the API in a loop needs to know before it bricks the tenant for the day.

novel_features.name: Denied-Reason Distribution
novel_features.description: Tokenize the free-text deniedReason field across all denied requests and emit a top-N word distribution.
novel_features.rationale: Operates locally on stored denied requests; the portal shows individual reasons but not a distribution.
novel_features.why_it_matters: For compliance review and tone-of-policy checks, surfacing the actual words used is faster than reading each row.

novel_features.name: Compliance Report
novel_features.description: Render audit entries for a user or computer over a window in a format suitable for auditors (CSV or markdown).
novel_features.rationale: Joins user/computer filter with time-range and converts to a deliverable format the live API does not produce.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Auditor evidence requests always want a single artifact; this generates it in one command.

novel_features.name: Endpoint Risk Score
novel_features.description: Score each endpoint by elevation frequency, local-admin count, and AbR client version recency.
novel_features.rationale: Composite metric over multiple tables in the local store; not a query the API exposes.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Lets an admin focus remediation effort on the endpoints most likely to be abused.

narrative.headline: Every Admin By Request portal action, plus a local SQLite mirror of audit, events, inventory and requests for ad-hoc SQL and offline search.
narrative.value_prop: Pull audit log, events, inventory and requests into a local store with one sync, then approve or deny elevations, generate offline PIN codes, and answer cross-resource questions (repeat requestors, agent-version drift, audit/event correlation) the portal does not surface.
narrative.auth_narrative: Set ADMINBYREQUEST_API_KEY in your environment. The CLI sends it via the apikey header. AbR enforces a 100,000-call daily quota per tenant; the CLI tracks calls locally and warns you before exhaustion via quota forecast.

quickstart.comment: Confirm key works and detect which AbR data center your tenant lives on.
Pull audit log, events, inventory and requests into the local SQLite store.
Surface pending elevation requests to approve or deny.
Identify users repeatedly requesting elevation over the last 30 days.
Check whether the current API usage pace will exceed the 100k daily quota.

troubleshoots.symptom: doctor reports HTTP 500 from every data center
troubleshoots.fix: API key may be wrong; verify ADMINBYREQUEST_API_KEY is the value from your portal Settings > Tenant Settings > Data > API Keys.
You hit the daily quota; AbR blocks the tenant until next business day. Run quota forecast next time to predict before it happens.

troubleshoots.symptom: PIN code endpoint returns General error
troubleshoots.fix: Pass the challenge code via --challenge; the bare endpoint without a challenge returns this error by design.

narrative.when_to_use: Use this CLI when you are administering an Admin By Request tenant from a terminal or AI agent: triaging pending requests in bulk, pulling audit data into a SIEM you have not yet wired up, generating offline PINs without opening the portal, or answering cross-resource questions (repeat requestors, agent-version drift) that the portal does not directly support.

recipes.title: Triage pending requests as JSON
recipes.explanation: Surface the pending elevation queue in agent-friendly JSON narrowed to the fields needed to decide. Approve or deny individual ids with `requests approve <id>` or `requests deny <id>`.

recipes.title: Compliance evidence for one user
recipes.command: adminbyrequest-pp-cli report compliance --since 2026-01-01 --user CHRISCOOMBES --format md > evidence.md
recipes.explanation: Render every audit log entry for a user since a date into a markdown report.

recipes.title: Find every device on an old client
recipes.explanation: Inventory drift filters the synced inventory snapshot for endpoints below the target client version.

recipes.title: Check today's API quota consumption
recipes.explanation: Local counter of API calls made today; the CLI tracks them in ~/.cache/adminbyrequest-pp-cli/http/ so this never costs an API call.

recipes.title: Audit-to-event timeline narrowed via --select
recipes.explanation: Joins audit entry to nearby events on the same computer; --select narrows the deeply nested response so the agent does not parse the full payload.

trigger_phrases: list pending admin by request elevations
check who keeps requesting admin elevation
sync admin by request audit log
